input.background: Suppose you want to test $H_0: \mu_1=\mu_2$ for the cholesterol illustrative example at $\alpha=$ 0.05 (two-sided) with $90 \%$ power. You are looking for a mean difference of $1 \mathrm{mmol} / \mathrm{L}$ and will use the pooled estimate of variance (about 0.25) as a reasonable estimate of $\sigma^2$. How many individuals must be studied to achieve these conditions?
input.question: Round the adjusted sample size $n$ to the nearest integer and determine the minimum number of individuals per group.

output.answer: Round $n=6.36$ up to seven. Resolve to use a minimum of seven individuals per group.

meta_info.caption: Sample size requirement for hypothesis test.
